ucs.title: [LLM] Flax Typhoon (Ethereal Panda) Sticky Keys backdoor — NLA disabled + accessibility-feature hijack
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Flax Typhoon RDP persistence chain documented by Microsoft (Aug 2023): disabling Network Level Authentication on RDP-Tcp and weaponising accessibility binaries (sethc.exe, utilman.exe, osk.exe, Magnify.exe) so an unauthenticated attacker on the RDP login screen can press Shift x5 to spawn SYSTEM cmd.exe. Catches both the file-replacement and IFEO-debugger variants Flax Typhoon has been observed using.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_path="*\\Terminal Server\\WinStations\\RDP-Tcp*" AND Registry.registry_value_name="UserAuthentication" AND Registry.registry_value_data IN ("0x00000000","0")) OR (Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\sethc.exe*" Registry.registry_value_name="Debugger") OR (Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\utilman.exe*" Registry.registry_value_name="Debugger") OR (Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\osk.exe*" Registry.registry_value_name="Debugger") OR (Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\Magnify.exe*" Registry.registry_value_name="Debugger") OR (Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\Narrator.exe*" Registry.registry_value_name="Debugger") by host Registry.user Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data Registry.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\System32\\sethc.exe","*\\System32\\utilman.exe","*\\System32\\osk.exe","*\\System32\\Magnify.exe","*\\System32\\Narrator.exe") Filesystem.action IN ("modified","created","renamed") by host Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` ] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Flax Typhoon Sticky Keys + NLA-disable persistence chain
let _lookback = 30d;
// 1. NLA disabled on RDP-Tcp listener (UserAuthentication = 0)
let _nla_disabled =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "UserAuthentication"
    | where RegistryValueData in ("0","0x0","0x00000000")
    | extend Signal = "NLA_Disabled";
// 2. IFEO debugger set on accessibility binary (cmd.exe / powershell.exe pivot)
let _ifeo_accessibility =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey matches regex @"(?i)\\Image File Execution Options\\(sethc|utilman|osk|magnify|narrator|displayswitch|atbroker)\.exe"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "Debugger"
    | extend Signal = "IFEO_Accessibility_Debugger";
// 3. Direct file replacement of accessibility binary in System32 (Flax Typhoon's preferred variant — sethc.exe -> cmd.exe copy)
let _accessibility_overwrite =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where FolderPath has_cs @"C:\Windows\System32\"
    | where FileName in~ ("sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","magnify.exe","narrator.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("TrustedInstaller.exe","msiexec.exe","TiWorker.exe","poqexec.exe","WerFault.exe")
    | extend Signal = "Accessibility_Binary_Overwrite",
             RegistryKey = "", RegistryValueName = "", RegistryValueData = FileName
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Signal, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain;
union isfuzzy=true
    (_nla_disabled | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Signal, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain),
    (_ifeo_accessibility | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Signal, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain),
    (_accessibility_overwrite)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"      // skip machine accounts
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Flax Typhoon Sticky Keys + NLA-disable persistence chain
let _lookback = 30d;
let _nla_disabled =
    ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
    | where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp"
    | where RegistryValue =~ "UserAuthentication"
    | where RegistryValueData in ("0","0x0","0x00000000")
    | extend Signal = "NLA_Disabled";
let _ifeo_accessibility =
    ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
    | where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey matches regex @"(?i)\\Image File Execution Options\\(sethc|utilman|osk|magnify|narrator|displayswitch|atbroker)\.exe"
    | where RegistryValue =~ "Debugger"
    | extend Signal = "IFEO_Accessibility_Debugger";
let _accessibility_overwrite =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where TargetFilePath has_cs @"C:\Windows\System32\"
    | where TargetFileName in~ ("sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","magnify.exe","narrator.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe")
    | where ActingProcessName !endswith_cs "TrustedInstaller.exe"
        and ActingProcessName !endswith_cs "msiexec.exe"
        and ActingProcessName !endswith_cs "TiWorker.exe"
    | extend Signal = "Accessibility_Binary_Overwrite",
             RegistryKey = "", RegistryValue = "", RegistryValueData = TargetFileName,
             DvcHostname = DvcHostname, ActorUsername = ActorUsername,
             ActingProcessCommandLine = ActingProcessCommandLine, ActingProcessName = ActingProcessName;
union isfuzzy=true
    (_nla_disabled | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, Signal, RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, ActorUsername),
    (_ifeo_accessibility | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, Signal, RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, ActorUsername),
    (_accessibility_overwrite | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, Signal, RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine, ActorUsername)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Flax Typhoon Accessibility Feature IFEO Debugger Hijack
id: 9c1f4a2e-5b1f-4d6a-9b3a-1f0e7a4c1d22
status: experimental
description: Detects Flax Typhoon's sticky-keys backdoor where an Image File Execution Options Debugger value is set on an accessibility binary (sethc.exe, utilman.exe, osk.exe, magnify.exe, narrator.exe), giving SYSTEM-level cmd.exe at the RDP login screen.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/08/24/flax-typhoon-using-legitimate-software-to-quietly-access-taiwanese-organizations/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - attack.t1546.008
  - attack.t1562.001
  - detection.flax_typhoon
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: registry_set
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|contains|all:
      - 'Image File Execution Options\'
      - '\Debugger'
    TargetObject|endswith:
      - 'sethc.exe\Debugger'
      - 'utilman.exe\Debugger'
      - 'osk.exe\Debugger'
      - 'magnify.exe\Debugger'
      - 'narrator.exe\Debugger'
      - 'displayswitch.exe\Debugger'
      - 'atbroker.exe\Debugger'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate debugging of accessibility binaries by Microsoft engineers (extremely rare in production)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's August 2023 Flax Typhoon report explicitly names this exact chain — disabling NLA on RDP-Tcp followed by replacing sethc.exe with cmd.exe — as the actor's hallmark persistence move on Taiwanese government and telecom targets. The combination of (a) a NLA toggle to 0 and (b) accessibility-binary tampering in the same host within days is essentially diagnostic of Flax Typhoon / Ethereal

ucs.title: [LLM] Flax Typhoon SoftEther VPN bridge masquerading as Windows binary (conhost/dllhost in C:\Windows\Fonts)
ucs.description: Hunts Flax Typhoon's signature C2/persistence: a renamed SoftEther vpnclient.exe / vpnbridge.exe / vpnserver.exe deployed to non-standard paths (commonly C:\Windows\Fonts\, C:\Users\Public\, C:\PerfLogs\) and disguised as conhost.exe / dllhost.exe / lsass.exe. The SoftEther command-line vocabulary (/server, /hub, /password, AccountCreate, AccountConnect, RemoteEnable) and PE metadata (Internal/Original filename = vpnclient/vpnbridge/vpncmd, company SoftEther) are reliable invariants even after rename.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as path values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ( Processes.original_file_name IN ("vpnclient.exe","vpnbridge.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpncmd.exe") OR Processes.process_name!=Processes.original_file_name AND Processes.original_file_name IN ("vpnclient.exe","vpnbridge.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpncmd.exe") OR (Processes.process IN ("*/server *","*/hub *","*/password:*","*AccountCreate *","*AccountConnect *","*RemoteEnable *","*BridgeCreate *","*HubCreate *") AND Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Windows\\Fonts\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")) ) by host Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.original_file_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Flax Typhoon SoftEther VPN bridge — masquerade + characteristic CLI flags
let _suspect_paths = dynamic([@"c:\windows\fonts\", @"c:\users\public\", @"c:\perflogs\", @"c:\programdata\", @"c:\windows\temp\", @"c:\windows\debug\"]);
let _softether_cli_tokens = dynamic(["/server","/hub","/password:","AccountCreate","AccountConnect","RemoteEnable","BridgeCreate","HubCreate","vpncmd","/CMD"]);
let _masquerade_names = dynamic(["conhost.exe","dllhost.exe","lsass.exe","svchost.exe","taskhostw.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let _renamed_softether =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where ActingProcessFileCompany has "SoftEther"
         or ActingProcessFileProduct has "SoftEther" or ActingProcessFileProduct has "PacketiX"
         or ActingProcessFileInternalName has_any ("vpnclient","vpnbridge","vpnserver","vpncmd")
         or ActingProcessFileOriginalName has_any ("vpnclient","vpnbridge","vpnserver","vpncmd")
    | where TargetProcessName !has_any ("vpnclient.exe","vpnbridge.exe","vpnserver.exe","vpncmd.exe")
         or tolower(TargetProcessName) has_any (_suspect_paths)
    | extend Signal = "SoftEther_Rename_Masquerade";
let _softether_cli =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (_softether_cli_tokens)
    | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("/server","AccountConnect","AccountCreate","vpncmd")
    | where tolower(TargetProcessName) has_any (_suspect_paths)
         or TargetProcessName has_any (_masquerade_names)
    | extend Signal = "SoftEther_CLI_Suspicious_Path";
union isfuzzy=true _renamed_softether, _softether_cli
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, Signal, ActorUsername,
          TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine,
          ActingProcessFileCompany, ActingProcessFileProduct,
          ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine, SHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Flax Typhoon SoftEther VPN Bridge Masquerade
id: 7e2b9f3a-44c1-4e2d-bd0c-2e9a3b8f01c4
status: experimental
description: Detects Flax Typhoon's renamed SoftEther vpnclient/vpnbridge/vpnserver binaries deployed to non-standard paths (C:\Windows\Fonts, C:\Users\Public) and/or invoked with SoftEther command-line vocabulary (/server, /hub, AccountConnect, vpncmd). PE original/internal filename and company name continue to expose the SoftEther origin even after on-disk rename.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/08/24/flax-typhoon-using-legitimate-software-to-quietly-access-taiwanese-organizations/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1090
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.t1133
  - detection.flax_typhoon
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  metadata_softether:
    OriginalFileName:
      - 'vpnclient.exe'
      - 'vpnbridge.exe'
      - 'vpnserver.exe'
      - 'vpncmd.exe'
  filename_renamed:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\conhost.exe'
      - '\dllhost.exe'
      - '\lsass.exe'
      - '\svchost.exe'
      - '\taskhostw.exe'
  cli_softether:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/server '
      - '/hub '
      - '/password:'
      - 'AccountConnect'
      - 'AccountCreate'
      - 'RemoteEnable'
      - 'vpncmd '
  path_suspicious:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Windows\Fonts\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '\Windows\debug\'
  condition: (metadata_softether and (filename_renamed or path_suspicious)) or (cli_softether and path_suspicious)
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate enterprise SoftEther VPN deployments (rare; should be allowlisted by signed-path)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Flax Typhoon (Microsoft) / Ethereal Panda (CrowdStrike) is uniquely characterised by abusing the legitimate, signed SoftEther VPN client (vpnclient.exe / vpnbridge.exe) as a long-haul reverse-bridge into the victim network — Microsoft's writeup specifically calls out renaming the binary to conhost.exe and placing it under C:\Windows\Fonts\. Other Chinese clusters (Volt Typhoon, APT41) prefer diffe
